context: Post-FF-002 fixture correction. FF-003 analysis confirmed 3 sub-modes. Ranking derived from taxonomy state, analysis results, and production relevance.

priority_order.name: Extended-field extraction failure (invoice_date + tax_amount)
priority_order.actionability: Two verifier sub-modes have identified root causes (ambiguous date parsing, tax regex capturing percentage). Both are potentially addressable by extraction prompt changes or verifier regex improvements.
priority_order.recommended_next_action: Expand gold dataset to 20+ records with invoice_date + tax_amount. Then evaluate: (1) requiring ISO dates in extraction prompts for DATE_AMBIGUOUS, (2) regex improvement for _TAX_ANCHOR_VALUE_RE for TAX_AMOUNT_MISMATCH.
priority_order.condition_for_action: Gold dataset expanded with sufficient date/tax test cases for statistically meaningful evaluation.
priority_order.open_question: INV-1074 passes verifier but fails arithmetic — consider reclassifying to FF-001/L1 overlap. If reclassified, FF-003 drops to 5 cases.

priority_order.name: Sparse-tag extraction failure (match_fail only)
priority_order.actionability: Root cause unknown. No diagnostic tags. Cannot design intervention without understanding the failure.
priority_order.recommended_next_action: Manual inspection of GLC-4005 and APX-5004 raw text and extraction attempts to identify failure mechanism.
priority_order.condition_for_action: Manual inspection completed and root cause documented.

priority_order.name: Table-format arithmetic corruption (synthetic data)
priority_order.actionability: Extraction is correct (verify_extraction passes 12/12). Blocker is synthetic data quality — line items do not sum to stated grand total. Not addressable by extraction prompt or verifier changes.
priority_order.recommended_next_action: Fix synthetic invoice generator to produce arithmetically consistent documents. Then reassess.

ranking_rationale: FF-003 ranks first because it has confirmed, potentially addressable failure mechanisms at L3 (evidence-binding). FF-004 ranks second despite being smaller because its root cause is unknown — manual inspection is a prerequisite for any further work. FF-001 ranks last because despite being the largest family (12 cases), it requires an external fix (synthetic data generator) that is outside the scope of extraction or verifier improvements.
